_comment: RECOVERED near-verbatim from the 2026-07-09 session transcript. Entity IDs resolved against registries/entities.json and confirmed by the founder 2026-07-17: boiling temperature of water = SK-ENT-000005; pressure = SK-ENT-000006; temperature scale = SK-ENT-000007; method = SK-ENT-000008. This file's SHA-256 (of its JCS form) is enumerated in the genesis record next to the admissibility map hash. Changes after genesis are governance records (machine-readable delta + effective-from).

semantics.match_rule: A requirement is satisfied iff at least one condition in the triple has property == the required property entity ID. Requirements constrain presence, never value; value correctness is the review's job.
enforcement: intake (pre-review): a proposal missing any applicable required condition never enters review; rejection cites the specific unmet requirement and this ruleset version (principled, citable refusal)
seal (validator, SCHEMA s11): the fact must satisfy the mandatory-condition map in force at its chain position (genesis state + all preceding governance records)
semantics.byte_impact: none - this ruleset never alters canonical serialization or any hash; it is pipeline/witness layer only
semantics.rule_composition: applicable requirements = derivation_type_rules[derivation.type] UNION requirements of every subject_rules entry whose subject matches the triple's subject

_derivation_type_notes.null: not applicable - derivation type is BLOCKED by the admissibility map; no intake rules are defined for it
_derivation_type_notes.statistical_analysis: DORMANT in v1: RING-2 PENDING types cannot enter review until the Ring 2 governance record exists; the rule is encoded now so the founding table is complete and witnessed, and binds automatically on activation
_derivation_type_notes.laboratory_measurement: intentionally empty at the type level - not every measurement is condition-dependent (G is not); condition-dependence is a property of the subject, hence subject_rules below

subject_rules.subject: SK-ENT-000005
subject_rules.rationale: condition-dependent property: the claim is undefined without ambient pressure and the temperature scale (e.g. ITS-90); model/frame is an explicit condition per SCHEMA s3.4

_subject_rules_notes: Fundamental-constant subjects (c, h, G, N_A, e, k) carry no entries: their claims are condition-independent by construction.
Closed-by-default: a quantity-entity flagged condition_dependent in the registry must have a subject_rules entry before any fact about it can enter review.
